analysis: Seven nights in a row above six and a half hours, with five of them clearing seven and a half — that's not luck, that's your routine doing exactly what it's supposed to do. The 8th was your standout, nearly eight hours with only 32 minutes awake, and your REM has been consistently strong all week, hitting two hours or just over it on three nights. Deep sleep is the one thing still holding back — most nights sitting under 40 minutes, which your body would love more of, but that's not a crisis, that's just the next thing. The awake time on the 10th crept up to over an hour, which is worth noticing without catastrophising — everything else that night still held up fine.
